notes: Korean Gen 2 uses a mixed single-byte and two-byte encoding.
Bytes 0x00-0x0B are two-byte character prefixes; the second byte determines the character.
0x00 and 0x0B have identical outputs; the game only naturally uses 0x0B pairs.
User-input strings use byte pairs; 0x0B 0xFF is used for space in user input.
Available Korean characters mostly match the 2,350 hangul syllables in KS X 1001.
5 additional syllables (뢔, 쌰, 쎼, 쓔, 쬬) are placed in otherwise empty slots.
Changes from KS X 1001: 겸/겹 swapped, 굄 replaced by 괻, 댜 removed, 돐 removed, 읊 replaced by 읆.
Does not include jamo used exclusively as final consonants.

control_characters.function: A "half" line break (moves the print position to the place one tile below the start of the current line).
Prints ᆨtzx (mojibake for 'こうげき' attack; appears to be a leftover from the English version code).
Prints the opposing Trainer's name (including their Trainer class). (Outside of battle, this may not terminate properly.)
Prompts the player to press a button, after which the top line of the text window is replaced by the bottom, the bottom line is cleared, and the print position moves to the start of the bottom line.
Shifts text in the dialogue box up a line (so the top line is “lost”) without a prompt or a pause.
Dialogue line break (moves the print position to the expected start of the second line in a standard dialogue box).
Prompts the player to press a button, after which the text window is cleared to make way for the following text.
Prompts the player to press a button, after which the top line of the text window is replaced by the bottom, the bottom line is cleared, and the print position moves to the start of the bottom line.
Marks the end of dialogue, with no visual prompt to the player.
Marks the end of dialogue, with a visual prompt to the player.
